title: phaseolus vulgaris subsp. aborigineus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris subsp. aborigineus, also known as the Australian kidney bean or bush bean, is a subspecies of the common bean plant (Phaseolus vulgaris) native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant's medicinal properties include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its high content of polyphenolic compounds and other bioactive molecules. The health benefits of consuming Phaseolus vulgaris subsp. aborigineus include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving blood sugar control, and providing essential nutrients like protein, fiber, and minerals. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying and crushing the leaves or beans, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
